# National ROW

> Eminent domain and condemnation consulting for property owners nationwide. National ROW represents property owners (never the condemning agency) to win the full compensation they are owed in highway, utility, transmission, pipeline, water, and every other kind of right-of-way or easement taking. On the property owner's side since the 1980s. National ROW is a consulting firm, not a law firm.

National ROW helps property owners whose land is being taken — in full or in part — for a public right-of-way or easement by a government agency, utility, transmission company, or pipeline company. We carry the condemnation process for the owner: we assemble the expert team (condemnation appraisers, land planners, relocation specialists) and coordinate independent licensed attorneys when a case needs them, document what the property and the damages are truly worth, and deal with the agency directly. We are a consulting firm, not a law firm, and we do not provide legal advice.

## Key facts
- Service area: United States (nationwide). Headquartered in Texas.
- In business since the 1980s — more than 35 years representing property owners.
- We represent property owners only — never the condemning agency, utility, or pipeline.
- Right-of-way and easement types: highway and road, electric transmission lines, pipelines, water and drainage, utility easements, access easements, telecom/fiber, and full (fee) takings.
- Phone: (469) 484-7960 and (956) 363-4144. Email: info@nationalrow.com

## Core guides
- Do I have to accept the first offer?: No. The agency's initial offer is a starting point, not a final number. Owners generally have the right to contest the valuation and pursue higher compensation for what is taken and for damage to what they keep.
- Partial takings and damages to the remainder: When only a strip is taken, the owner may also be owed compensation for the lost value of the land they keep — often the largest and most overlooked part of a claim.
- Easement vs. fee taking: An easement lets a utility or pipeline use a strip while the owner keeps title (with restrictions); a fee taking transfers ownership. Both are compensable, and the easement terms matter.
- Just compensation: Generally the fair market value of what is taken plus the reduction in value to what remains.
- Is National ROW a law firm?: No. National ROW is a right-of-way and condemnation consulting firm, not a law firm, and does not provide legal advice; it coordinates independent licensed attorneys when needed.

## Notable projects
National ROW has represented property owners on major right-of-way and condemnation projects nationwide, including: The Purple Line light rail (Maryland & Washington, D.C.); President George W. Bush Turnpike (Dallas-Fort Worth, TX); Matterhorn Express Pipeline (TX); Duke Energy Modernization (NC); Galveston Seawall (TX); I-44 Interchange Expansion (Tulsa, OK); Ports-to-Plains / I-27 and Forts-to-Ports / I-14 (TX); Harbor Bridge (Corpus Christi, TX); Project Connect (Austin, TX); SH 99 Grand Parkway (Houston, TX); I-35 Capital Express (Austin, TX); Palmetto Pipeline (GA/SC/FL); Sabal Trail Pipeline (AL); and the US/Mexico Border Wall (TX).

## Contact
- Case review and contact form: https://nationalrow.com/#lead
- Phone: tel:+14694847960 or tel:+19563634144


## FAQ

- What does National ROW cost?: National ROW works on a contingency or flat-fee basis depending on the project. There is no upfront cost for most property owners — fees are earned only when we secure additional compensation. Contact us for a free case review.
- Do I have to sell my property if the government wants it?: If a government agency or utility has the legal right of eminent domain or condemnation, they can ultimately compel a taking — but you always have the right to negotiate the compensation amount and challenge the offer. You do not have to accept the first offer.
- What states does National ROW serve?: National ROW serves property owners across the entire United States. The firm is headquartered in Texas but handles cases in all 50 states.
- How long does the condemnation process take?: Timelines vary widely depending on the agency, project scope, and whether the case goes to trial. Simple cases may resolve in a few months; complex pipeline or highway cases can take a year or more. National ROW guides owners through every stage.
- What types of projects does National ROW handle?: Highway and road expansions, electric transmission lines, oil and gas pipelines, water and sewer lines, railroad corridors, fiber and telecom lines, and any other public or private right-of-way or easement taking.
- Can National ROW help if I already accepted an offer?: Yes. In many states you can still negotiate or challenge the compensation even after signing initial paperwork, as long as the final order has not been entered. Contact National ROW as soon as possible.
- What is a partial taking?: A partial taking occurs when only a portion of a property is acquired. The owner may be entitled to compensation not only for the land taken but also for damages to the remaining property — called severance damages.
- What is just compensation?: Just compensation is the legal standard for what a property owner must receive in an eminent domain taking. It generally equals the fair market value of the property or rights taken, plus any reduction in value caused to the remainder of the property.
- Will National ROW go to trial if needed?: Yes. National ROW assembles the expert team — appraisers, land planners, relocation specialists, and attorneys — required to take a case to condemnation trial if a fair settlement cannot be reached.
- How do I get started with National ROW?: Request a free case review at https://nationalrow.com/#lead or call (469) 484-7960 or (956) 363-4144. A specialist will evaluate your situation at no cost or obligation.


## Service pages
- Highway & DOT Condemnation: https://nationalrow.com/services/highway-condemnation/ — State DOTs and highway agencies must follow strict condemnation procedures. National ROW helps landowners maximize compensation when a road project takes their property.
- Pipeline Easement Compensation: https://nationalrow.com/services/pipeline-easement/ — Pipeline companies routinely undervalue easements. National ROW reviews pipeline easement offers and negotiates the full value of the rights being granted.
- Transmission Line Easement: https://nationalrow.com/services/transmission-line-easement/ — Electric utilities acquiring transmission line easements must pay fair compensation. National ROW represents landowners in negotiating with utilities.
- Solar & Wind Farm Easement: https://nationalrow.com/services/solar-wind-easement/ — Solar and wind developers lease large tracts at fixed rates that may be below market. National ROW helps landowners evaluate and negotiate energy easement agreements.
- Transit & Rail Condemnation: https://nationalrow.com/services/transit-rail-condemnation/ — Light rail, commuter rail, and freight rail projects take corridors through developed areas. National ROW assists affected property owners.

## State pages
- Texas Eminent Domain (Texas Property Code Ch. 21, TxDOT): https://nationalrow.com/states/texas/
- Arizona Eminent Domain (ARS Title 12 Ch. 11): https://nationalrow.com/states/arizona/
- North Carolina Eminent Domain (NCGS Ch. 40A): https://nationalrow.com/states/north-carolina/
- Florida Eminent Domain (Florida Statutes Ch. 73 & 74): https://nationalrow.com/states/florida/
- Georgia Eminent Domain (OCGA Title 22): https://nationalrow.com/states/georgia/
- Oklahoma Eminent Domain (Oklahoma Title 27A, 66, 69): https://nationalrow.com/states/oklahoma/
- Colorado Eminent Domain (CRS Title 38 Article 1): https://nationalrow.com/states/colorado/
